100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 18, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as a general strike by coal miners continued, impacting the nation’s economy and daily life. In the United States, the Senate moved forward with discussions on the controversial tariff bill, raising concerns among various industries. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political landscape grew increasingly volatile as tensions rose in the aftermath of the Treaty of Versailles.
